NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. When the LORD brought back the captivity of Zion, We were like those who dream.
NKJV.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, And our tongue with singing. Then they said among the nations, “The LORD has done great things for them.”
NKJV.3: The LORD has done great things for us, And we are glad.
NKJV.4: Bring back our captivity, O LORD, As the streams in the South.
NKJV.5: Those who sow in tears Shall reap in joy.
NKJV.6: He who continually goes forth weeping, Bearing seed for sowing, Shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, Bringing his sheaves with him.

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S>. When the LORD<S>3068</S> turned again<S>7725</S> the captivity<S>7870</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>, we were like them that dream<S>2492</S>. <sup>turned: Heb. returned the returning</sup>
KJV.2: Then was our mouth<S>6310</S> filled<S>4390</S> with laughter<S>7814</S>, and our tongue<S>3956</S> with singing<S>7440</S>: then said<S>559</S> they among the heathen<S>1471</S>, The LORD<S>3068</S> hath done<S>6213</S> great things<S>1431</S> for them. <sup>hath: Heb. hath magnified to do with them</sup>
KJV.3: The LORD<S>3068</S> hath done<S>6213</S> great things<S>1431</S> for us; whereof we are glad<S>8056</S>.
KJV.4: Turn again<S>7725</S> our captivity<S>7622</S> <S>7622</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, as the streams<S>650</S> in the south<S>5045</S>.
KJV.5: They that sow<S>2232</S> in tears<S>1832</S> shall reap<S>7114</S> in joy<S>7440</S>. <sup>joy: or, singing</sup>
KJV.6: He that goeth<S>3212</S> forth<S>1980</S> and weepeth<S>1058</S>, bearing<S>5375</S> precious<S>4901</S> seed<S>2233</S>, shall doubtless<S>935</S> come<S>935</S> again with rejoicing<S>7440</S>, bringing<S>5375</S> his sheaves<S>485</S> with him. <sup>precious: or, seed basket</sup>

ESV.1: When the LORD restored the fortunes of Zion, we were like those who dream.
ESV.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with shouts of joy; then they said among the nations, “The LORD has done great things for them.”
ESV.3: The LORD has done great things for us; we are glad.
ESV.4: Restore our fortunes, O LORD, like streams in the Negeb!
ESV.5: Those who sow in tears shall reap with shouts of joy!
ESV.6: He who goes out weeping, bearing the seed for sowing, shall come home with shouts of joy, bringing his sheaves with him.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. When the LORD brought back the captive ones of Zion, We were like those who dream.
NASB.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter And our tongue with joyful shouting; Then they said among the nations, 'The LORD has done great things for them.'
NASB.3: The LORD has done great things for us; We are glad.
NASB.4: Restore our captivity, O LORD, As the streams in the South.
NASB.5: Those who sow in tears shall reap with joyful shouting.
NASB.6: He who goes to and fro weeping, carrying [his] bag of seed, Shall indeed come again with a shout of joy, bringing his sheaves [with him].

NLT.1:
When the LORD brought back his exiles to Jerusalem,
it was like a dream!
NLT.2:
We were filled with laughter,
and we sang for joy.
And the other nations said,
“What amazing things the LORD has done for them.”
NLT.3:
Yes, the LORD has done amazing things for us!
What joy!
NLT.4:
Restore our fortunes, LORD,
as streams renew the desert.
NLT.5:
Those who plant in tears
will harvest with shouts of joy.
NLT.6:
They weep as they go to plant their seed,
but they sing as they return with the harvest.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. When Yahweh brought back those who returned to Zion, we were like those who dream.
WEB.2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing. Then they said among the nations, “Yahweh has done great things for them.”
WEB.3: Yahweh has done great things for us, and we are glad.
WEB.4: Restore our fortunes again, Yahweh, like the streams in the Negev.
WEB.5: Those who sow in tears will reap in joy.
WEB.6: He who goes out weeping, carrying seed for sowing, will certainly come again with joy, carrying his sheaves.

CSB17.1:

we were like those who dream. ⓚ
CSB17.2:

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. When the LORD brought back the captives to Zion (Jerusalem),
We were like those who dream [it seemed so unreal].
AMP.2:
Then our mouth was filled with laughter
And our tongue with joyful shouting;
Then they said among the nations,
"The LORD has done great things for them."
AMP.3:
The LORD has done great things for us;
We are glad!
AMP.4:
Restore our captivity, O LORD,
As the stream-beds in the South (the Negev) [are restored by torrents of rain].
AMP.5: They who sow in tears shall reap with joyful singing.
AMP.6:
He who goes back and forth weeping, carrying his bag of seed [for planting],
Will indeed come again with a shout of joy, bringing his sheaves with him.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
It seemed like a dream, too good to be true,
when God returned Zion's exiles.
MSG.2:
We laughed, we sang,
we couldn't believe our good fortune.
We were the talk of the nations—
"God was wonderful to them!"
MSG.3:
God was wonderful to us;
we are one happy people.
MSG.4:
And now, God, do it again—
bring rains to our drought-stricken lives
MSG.5:
So those who planted their crops in despair
will shout hurrahs at the harvest,
MSG.6:
So those who went off with heavy hearts
will come home laughing, with armloads of blessing.

NIV.1:
Psalm 126
A song of ascents.
When the Lord brought back the captives to Zion,
we were like men who dreamed.
NIV.2:
Our mouths were filled with laughter,
our tongues with songs of joy.
Then it was said among the nations,
“The Lord has done great things for them.”
NIV.3:
The Lord has done great things for us,
and we are filled with joy.
NIV.4:
Restore our fortunes, O Lord,
like streams in the Negev.
NIV.5:
Those who sow in tears
will reap with songs of joy.
NIV.6:
He who goes out weeping,
carrying seed to sow,
will return with songs of joy,
carrying sheaves with him.

NRSVCE.1:
When the LORD restored the fortunes of Zion,
we were like those who dream.
NRSVCE.2:
Then our mouth was filled with laughter,
and our tongue with shouts of joy;
then it was said among the nations,
“The LORD has done great things for them.”
NRSVCE.3:
The LORD has done great things for us,
and we rejoiced.
NRSVCE.4:
Restore our fortunes, O LORD,
like the watercourses in the Negeb.
NRSVCE.5:
May those who sow in tears
reap with shouts of joy.
NRSVCE.6:
Those who go out weeping,
bearing the seed for sowing,
shall come home with shouts of joy,
carrying their sheaves.

NET.1: When the LORD restored the well-being of Zion, we thought we were dreaming.
NET.2: At that time we laughed loudly and shouted for joy. At that time the nations said, “The LORD has accomplished great things for these people.”
NET.3: The LORD did indeed accomplish great things for us. We were happy.
NET.4: O LORD, restore our well-being, just as the streams in the arid south are replenished.
NET.5: Those who shed tears as they plant will shout for joy when they reap the harvest.
NET.6: The one who weeps as he walks along, carrying his bag of seed, will certainly come in with a shout of joy, carrying his sheaves of grain.
